metadata.notes: Wingspan standard overhaul: removed legacy sections format, added rules_citations with key rulings, validated content against official rulebook.

subtopics.title: Components
subtopics.content:
#### Boards & Mats
- 1 game board — 4 advancement tracks along edges (Exploration, Science, Technology, Military) plus central territory map for hexagonal tiles
- 16 civilization mats — each with a unique asymmetric ability
- 5 income mats — tracks income across 5 eras with rows of symbols that unlock as you place buildings
- 5 capital city mats — each with a unique 9-column grid for placing landmarks and buildings

#### Cards
- 43 tapestry cards — played at the start of each era for ongoing or immediate benefits
- 48 territory tiles — hexagonal pieces gained from the Exploration track, placed on the central map
- 33 tech cards — double-sided (basic on front, upgraded on back), gained from Science track and flipped at upgrade spaces

#### Miniatures & Buildings
- 18 landmark miniatures — 3D plastic buildings in various shapes (L-pieces, T-pieces, straight bars), earned from advancement tracks and placed on your capital city grid
- 13 income buildings per player — small wooden cubes in player color, placed on income mat to expose new income symbols

#### Tokens & Resources
- Resource tokens in 4 types: Food (green), Workers (orange/brown), Gold (yellow), Culture (purple/blue) — max 8 of each, excess is lost
- VP tokens in denominations of 1, 5, 10, and 20
- 4 track marker tokens per player + 1 score marker per player

#### Other
- 1 rulebook, 1 reference guide, 5 player reference cards, 1 first-player token

subtopics.title: Initial Setup
subtopics.content:
#### Setting Up the Game
1. **Place the game board** — center of the table.
2. **Place landmarks** — put all 18 landmark miniatures on their marked spaces along the advancement tracks (first-come, first-served rewards).
3. **Shuffle territory tiles** — face-down supply pile near the board.
4. **Shuffle card decks** — tapestry cards and tech cards into two separate face-down decks.
5. **Sort income buildings** — by type, place in a supply near the board.
6. **Deal civilization mats** — 2 per player; choose 1, return the other face-down.
7. **Take player mats** — each player takes 1 income mat and 1 capital city mat.
8. **Deal starting cards** — 3 tapestry cards and 3 tech cards to each player.
9. **Place track markers** — all 4 player markers at the start (bottom) of the advancement tracks.

#### First Income Turn
- All players simultaneously take their first income turn before the game begins
- **Gain starting resources** — from the leftmost column on your income mat (Food, Workers, Gold, and Culture)
- **Resolve civilization abilities** — some civilizations modify setup or starting resources
- **Determine first player** — randomly

#### Ready Check
- Civilization mat chosen and placed in front of you
- Income mat and capital city mat in front of you
- Markers at the start of all 4 tracks
- 3 tapestry cards and 3 tech cards in hand
- Starting resources from the first income turn

subtopics.title: Turn Structure
subtopics.content:
#### Turn Options
On your turn, choose exactly 1 of 2 actions:

**Option 1: Advance Turn**
- **Choose a track** — pick any of the 4 advancement tracks (Exploration, Science, Technology, Military).
- **Pay the cost** — spend the resource(s) shown on the next space up.
- **Move your marker** — advance 1 space and immediately gain the benefit printed on that space.
- **Claim landmarks** — if you land on a landmark space, take that 3D miniature and place it on your capital city mat.
- **No track restrictions** — advance on any track in any order across your turns.

**Option 2: Income Turn**
1. **Play a tapestry card** — optional but important; gives bonuses for the upcoming era.
2. **Activate civilization ability** — if it triggers on income turns.
3. **Gain income** — from all exposed symbols on your income mat.
- Taking an income turn advances you to the next era. You take exactly 5 income turns over the game (one per era).

#### Asynchronous Pacing
- **Players do not need to be in the same era** — you might take your 3rd income turn while others are still advancing in Era 2.
- **Game end** — the game ends when ALL players have taken their 5th and final income turn. Players who finish early wait.

#### Important Timing Rules
- **Tapestry cards before income** — played BEFORE you gain income, so you cannot use incoming resources to pay card costs.
- **Landmarks placed immediately** — must go on your capital city mat the moment they are earned.
- **Income buildings placed immediately** — must go on your income mat the moment they are gained.

subtopics.title: The Four Advancement Tracks
subtopics.content:
#### Exploration Track (Primary cost: Food)
- **Gain territory tiles** — hexagonal pieces placed on the central map, adjacent to existing tiles.
- **Explore face-down** — some spaces let you draw a random tile; others let you choose from face-up tiles.
- **Collect tile resources** — many tiles have resources printed on them, collected immediately on placement.
- **Map control** — territory tiles expand the shared map and can earn control bonuses.

#### Science Track (Primary cost: Workers)
- **Gain tech cards** — drawn from the tech deck as you advance.
- **Upgrade spaces** — flip tech cards from basic side to powerful upgraded side.
- **Upgraded tech cards** — provide strong ongoing abilities or end-game VP.
- **Best as mid-game investment** — push into Science once you know which upgrades help your strategy most.

#### Technology Track (Primary cost: Gold)
- **Gain income buildings** — placed on your income mat, exposing new income symbols.
- **Economic engine** — each building placed means better income on every future income turn.
- **5 building types** — matching the 5 columns on your income mat.
- **Compound returns** — getting buildings early compounds across multiple eras.

#### Military Track (Primary cost: Culture)
- **Place outposts** — on territories you control.
- **Conquer territories** — topple an opponent's outpost on an adjacent territory (their outpost stays but is tipped over).
- **Direct VP and map control** — strongest in mid-to-late game when the map has plenty of territories to contest.

subtopics.title: Capital City & Scoring
subtopics.content:
#### Capital City Mat
- **9-column grid** — spatial puzzle where you place landmarks and income buildings throughout the game.
- **Landmarks** — 3D miniatures from advancement tracks; come in shapes: 1x1, L-shaped, T-shaped, straight bars. Harder to fit.
- **Income buildings** — simple 1x1 cubes that fill single spaces. Use to patch gaps landmarks cannot reach.
- **No overlapping** — pieces cannot overlap or extend outside the grid boundaries.

#### City Scoring Rules
- **Completed rows** — score the VP value shown at that row's edge.
- **Completed columns** — score the VP value shown at the column's top.
- **Partial rows/columns score zero** — even one empty space means no points for that line.
- **All-or-nothing** — completing both rows AND columns is the key to a high capital city score.

#### Placement Strategy
- **Place landmarks first** — they are bigger and harder to fit; place before filling gaps with 1x1 income buildings.
- **Study your mat during setup** — each capital city mat has a different grid pattern with pre-filled spaces.
- **Plan ahead** — placing landmarks carelessly early can block high-value rows later.

subtopics.title: Endgame & Final Scoring
subtopics.content:
#### Game End
The game ends when all players have taken their 5th and final income turn. Players who finish early wait for others to complete their turns.

#### VP Sources
1. **VP tokens** — earned during the game from track advances, tapestry card effects, conquest bonuses, tech card abilities, and civilization powers.
2. **Capital city scoring** — VP for every completely filled row and column on your capital city grid. Partial rows/columns score zero.
3. **Upgraded tech card bonuses** — some award end-game VP based on accomplishments.
4. **Tapestry card bonuses** — some give VP based on final game state.
5. **Territory control** — points for territories you control.

#### Tiebreaker
Most remaining resources. Highest total VP wins.

subtopics.title: Beginner Strategy
subtopics.content:
#### Starting Principles
- **Focus on 2 tracks, not 4.** Spreading across all 4 tracks dilutes everything. Pick 2 that synergize and push deep — the further you climb, the better the rewards.
- **Technology track is king for beginners.** Income buildings grow your economy. Each one exposes new income symbols, compounding across every remaining era.
- **Don't take income turns too early.** Every income turn means one fewer era of advancing. Stretch resources as far as possible before taking income.
- **Plan capital city placement from the start.** Study upcoming landmark shapes and where they fit. Partial rows score zero — place carefully.
- **Save strong tapestry cards for later eras.** "2 VP per advance this era" scores 4 VP with 2 advances but 12 VP with 6. Your engine is bigger later.
- **Gold is your emergency fund.** Save it for when you are one resource short of a critical advance.
- **Read your civilization ability carefully.** Your civ power shapes your entire strategy. Build your game plan around it.

#### Common Beginner Mistakes
- Taking income too early — you lose potential advance turns that build your engine
- Ignoring the Technology track — income buildings ARE your economic engine
- Placing landmarks poorly on the capital city grid, blocking future rows and columns
- Spreading evenly across all 4 tracks instead of focusing on 2
- Not reading tapestry card timing — some say "this era" and some say "when played," and the difference matters
- Hoarding resources instead of advancing — resources don't score directly, advances do
- Racing for a landmark that someone else will reach first — pivot to a different track instead of wasting resources

subtopics.title: First-Game Walkthrough
subtopics.content:
#### Your First Game of Tapestry

**Setup Decision**: Pick a civilization from the 2 dealt to you. For your first game, choose straightforward abilities: Historians (VP for each advance) or Craftsmen (capital city bonuses). Avoid rule-changers like The Chosen or Inventors until you know the base game.

**Era 1: Setup Income Turn**
- Everyone takes their first income turn simultaneously
- No tapestry card yet — save them for later eras
- Gain income from the leftmost column of your income mat (starting resources)
- Check your civilization ability — some give bonus resources or cards at setup
- You should have: Food, Workers, Gold, Culture + 3 tapestry cards + 3 tech cards

**Your First Advance Turns**

*Turn 1*: Advance on the Technology track. Pay the cost, gain an income building, place it on your income mat. You just unlocked better income for your next income turn.

*Turn 2*: Advance on your second focus track. Exploration is great for beginners — territory tiles come with free resources printed on them.

*Turn 3*: Continue advancing on one of your two focus tracks. If a landmark is within 1-2 spaces, push for it — they are first-come, first-served.

**The Landmark Race**
- When you reach a landmark space, take the 3D miniature and place it on your capital city mat immediately
- Landmarks are big and oddly shaped — position them where they help complete full rows and columns

**When to Take Your Era 2 Income Turn**
1. **Play a tapestry card** — if you have a good one for this era.
2. **Activate civilization ability** — if applicable.
3. **Collect income** — from all exposed symbols on your income mat. Should be noticeably better than Era 1 thanks to income buildings.

**Eras 3-5: The Engine Grows**
- Each era, income grows thanks to more income buildings
- Use your growing resource base to advance further on your focus tracks
- **Sweet spot** — 4-6 advances per era
- **Late-game tapestry cards become huge** — "Gain 1 VP per space advanced" might be worth 15-30 VP in Era 5

**The Final Income Turn**
- Your 5th income turn ends your game
- Score your capital city grid — only completely filled rows and columns count
- Add VP tokens + end-game bonuses from tech cards and tapestry cards

**First Game Target Score**: 150-200 VP. Focus on the advance-then-income rhythm and how income buildings power your engine.

subtopics.title: Advanced Strategy
subtopics.content:
#### Civilization Tier Guide
- **Tier S (Overpowered pre-nerf):** Futurists (79% win rate at 3p before nerfs), Isolationists (score for largest connected landmass — strong with Exploration + Military).
- **Tier A (Strong):** Historians (scale with player count, reward passive play), Craftsmen (30+ VP from civ mat scoring), Merrymakers (straightforward scoring, hard to mess up).
- **Tier B (Solid):** Militants (Military focus), Traders (resource flexibility, better at 3-5p), Inventors (extra tech upgrades), The Chosen (Science synergy).
- **Tier C (Situational):** Mystics (high variance), Architects (strong at 2p, weak at 5p), Nomads (need both Exploration and Military).

#### Track Pairings & Resource Costs
- **Exploration** — costs Food
- **Science** — costs Workers
- **Technology** — costs Gold
- **Military** — costs Culture
- **Best pairings:** Exploration + Military (place tiles then conquer them), Technology + Exploration (engine building + free tile resources), Science as a mid-game accelerator added to any 2-track base.

#### The Income Building Secret
- **Only the 1st, 3rd, and 4th income buildings per type generate future income** — the 2nd building is a dead placement unless you get the 3rd out before your next income turn.
- **Highest-impact skill:** get 2-3 income buildings out BEFORE your first income turn. Each early building compounds roughly 3 extra resources over the game.

#### The Exploration Rush
- **Strongest known opening:** advance Exploration 4 times. Territory tiles give resources when placed, making many advances net-zero cost.
- **Then advance Military twice** — conquer for resources and pick up a 2nd income building.
- **Result:** 2 buildings out before your first income turn, giving a huge economic lead.

#### Player Count Adjustments
- **2 Players** — city strategy dominates; Military is weak (less to conquer).
- **3-4 Players** — landmark racing becomes real; plan track choices around available landmarks. Historians get strong.
- **5 Players** — city completion less reliable with more landmark competition. Military and territory control dominate (crowded map).

#### Key Decisions by Era
1. **Era 1** — get income buildings placed before your first income turn (highest-impact decision in the game).
2. **Era 2-3** — commit to your 2 focus tracks and push deep.
3. **Era 3** — the pivotal decision: one more advance vs. taking income? Getting this timing right separates good players from great ones.
4. **Era 4** — play your best tapestry card (highest impact here).
5. **Era 5** — close out capital city rows for 10+ VP each. Every action must score.

key_rulings.text: When you reach a landmark space on an advancement track, you take that landmark miniature. If another player has already taken it, you do not receive a landmark. Each landmark can only be claimed by one player.
On an income turn, you first play a tapestry card (optional), then activate your civilization ability, and then gain income. The tapestry card is played BEFORE you receive your income resources.

key_rulings.topic: Asynchronous pacing — players in different eras
key_rulings.text: Players do not need to be in the same era. One player may take their 3rd income turn while others are still advancing. The game ends only when ALL players have completed their 5th income turn.

key_rulings.topic: Income buildings must be placed immediately
key_rulings.text: When you gain an income building from an advancement track, you must place it on your income mat immediately. You cannot hold it in reserve.

key_rulings.topic: Capital city partial rows score zero
key_rulings.text: Only fully completed rows and columns on your capital city mat score victory points. A row or column with even one empty space scores zero points.

key_rulings.topic: Gold is not a wild resource
key_rulings.text: Gold is one of the four resource types (Food, Workers, Gold, Culture). It is not wild and cannot substitute for other resources unless a specific ability allows it.

key_rulings.topic: Landmarks must be placed on capital city immediately
key_rulings.text: When you gain a landmark from an advancement track, you must place it on your capital city mat immediately. Plan ahead — a poorly placed landmark can block future rows and columns.

key_rulings.topic: Conquer dice — choose result in later eras
key_rulings.text: When conquering via the Military track, you roll the conquer die. In the first era, you must take the rolled result. In later eras, you may choose between the rolled result and VP.
You can have at most 8 of each resource (coins, workers, food, and culture are resources). Any resources gained beyond 8 of a single type are lost. Victory points are NOT resources and have no cap.

key_rulings.topic: No actions after your final income turn
key_rulings.text: You may not respond to other players (e.g., tapestry cards) after your final income turn. Your active game is over. You may still passively gain VP from civilization abilities that trigger on other players' actions, but you cannot actively play cards or spend resources.

key_rulings.topic: Benefits and landmarks are mandatory; bonuses are optional
key_rulings.text: All bonuses in the game are optional. Benefits and landmarks are mandatory rewards. When you advance on a track, you must gain the benefit shown on the space (if possible) and must take any landmark earned. However, bonuses (the extra cost/reward shown below certain spaces) are always optional.

key_rulings.topic: Cannot activate same track space twice per turn
key_rulings.text: You may not activate the same space on an advancement track more than once during your turn. If civilization abilities or card effects push you back and allow you to advance again, you cannot re-activate a space already visited this turn.

key_rulings.topic: Conquer and topple — territory with 2 tokens is permanently unconquerable
key_rulings.text: When you conquer a territory controlled by an opponent, you place your outpost and topple the opponent's outpost. A territory with 2 tokens (in any combination of standing/toppled) cannot be conquered again by anyone. A toppled outpost still counts for adjacency purposes.

The income turn must be executed in order: 1) Activate civilization ability, 2) Play a tapestry card (skip on first income turn), 3) Upgrade one technology card (optional), 4) Score VP from exposed icons, 5) Gain income from exposed resource icons. Performing steps out of order is a common mistake.
